results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration - arXiv
results.content:
The first design choice is to key every view by a globally unique node identifier rather than by canonical name. Surface-form alignment, the natural alternative, has a worse asymptotic cost and exposes the system to false merges whenever distinct entities share the same string. The following proposition makes both observations precise.

###### Proposition 1 (Identifier Preserving Joins). [...] Unified scientific knowledge infrastructure. We introduce Agents-K1, an agent-native knowledge orchestration pipeline that unifies KG, LLM, and CLI in a unified framework. Unlike static scholarly knowledge graphs, Agents-K1 is designed around the full workflow of research agents: parsing papers, extracting structured knowledge, building reusable graphs, and exposing the resulting knowledge through agent-facing interfaces. [...] Agents-K1 is designed as a reusable knowledge infrastructure for this setting. Instead of asking the agent to reason directly over scattered PDFs and flat search results, Agents-K1 first converts documents into structured multimodal knowledge graphs, then exposes these graphs through retrieval and tool interfaces that an agent can use during research. In this way, the framework separates two jobs that are often mixed together: offline construction of reliable knowledge representations, and online use of this knowledge for evidence-grounded reasoning.

results.title: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration - Paper Detail
results.content:
Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction [...] ### Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration

 2026-06-11 17:58:35
 Zongsheng Cao, Bihao Zhan, Jinxin Shi, Jiong Wang, Fangchen Yu, Zhijie Zhong, Zijie Guo, Tianshuo Peng, Zhuo Liu, Yi Xie, Xiang Zhuang, Yue Fan, Runmin Ma, Shiyang Feng, Xiangchao Yan, Anran Liu, Peng Ye, Wenlong Zhang, Shufei Zhang, Chunfeng Song, Fenghua Ling, Jie Zhou, Liang He, Bo Zhang, Lei Bai
 0

### Abstract [...] rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.

results.title: Top 9 Microsoft AI Agent Alternatives for Employees in 2025
results.content:
Microsoft AI Agents can be a great addition to your team. However, if speed, accuracy, and customization are your priorities, then the alternatives we listed in this article are worth your while.

Let’s recap: the top 9 Microsoft AI Agent alternatives for employees are:

1. Capacity
2. Kore.ai
3. Guru
4. Perplexity.AI
5. Glean
6. Leena AI
7. Rasa
8. Amazon Q
9. Moveworks

If you’ve come this far, you’re probably still looking for something better—a tool that offers not only assistance features for your team but also automates customer support, connects data across your entire business, integrates with hundreds of other platforms, and uses conversational AI that maintains brand guidelines. If that’s the case, then it sounds like you’d be a perfect fit to try Capacity. [...] Get the guidebook

ChatGPT Perplexity Google AI

TL;DR

 Microsoft AI agents automate workflows, retrieve files, and assist employees across Microsoft 365 apps.
 They handle tasks like FAQs, meeting summaries, and coding support.
 Top alternatives to Microsoft AI agents in 2025 include Capacity, Kore.ai, Guru, Perplexity.AI, Glean, Leena AI, Rasa, Amazon Q, and Moveworks.
 Choose a platform based on speed, accuracy, integrations, and team needs. [...] Workflow automation: From email automations to bookings, refunds, policy FAQs, and many other operations—Capacity can handle it all without bothering your team. This allows them to focus on the more important parts of their work.
 Agent assist: AI agents for employees help your team at every step of their work to make them more productive and reduce stress. The platform offers your agents helpful features like AI-assisted real-time suggestions, coaching, post-call summaries, and other functionalities to do their job more efficiently.

results.title: Top 5 Agent Development Kit Google Alternatives: Explore AI Agent Frameworks Like Google ADK
results.content:
- Simular AI's Agent S
   An open-source framework for building AI agents that interact autonomously with computers through graphical user interfaces (GUIs), ideal for tasks like form filling or product searches.
   URL: Agent S GitHub
- OpenAI Agents SDK
   A lightweight, open-source framework for multi-agent workflows, enabling developers to build agentic AI applications with minimal abstractions and built-in tracing for debugging.
   URL: OpenAI Agents SDK
- Amazon Agents on Bedrock
   A fully managed, proprietary service for building AI agents, offering orchestration capabilities and integration with AWS, suitable for enterprise-scale applications.
   URL: Amazon Bedrock Agents
- LangChain [...] URL: Amazon Bedrock Agents
- LangChain
   An open-source framework for LLM-powered applications, including AI agents, with a modular architecture and support for external integrations like APIs and databases.
   URL: LangChain GitHub
- AutoGen
   An open-source framework from Microsoft for multi-agent AI systems, enabling complex conversations between agents and supporting scalability for enterprise use.
   URL: AutoGen GitHub [...] Open-source frameworks empower technical teams to craft precise workflows through customizable code. Real-world successes in logistics and healthcare prove these tools deliver measurable efficiency gains when paired with rigorous evaluation methods.

Key technical advantages include streamlined cloud infrastructure integration and adaptive learning capabilities. Developers gain granular control over deployment pipelines while maintaining enterprise-grade security protocols across distributed systems.

For teams ready to innovate, start by experimenting with sandbox environments. Review documentation for popular libraries that support multi-stage testing and real-time debugging. These resources help bridge the gap between prototyping and production-ready implementations.

| Interleaved RL reasoning: +12.5% Pass@1, −37% reasoning length, −80% TTFT vs. think-then-act; consistent across PPO, GRPO, REINFORCE++ | arXiv:2505.19640, Jan 2026 update | B — multi-RL validation, preprint |
| AgentBeats: standardized reproducible multi-agent evaluation — 298 judge agents, 467 subject agents, 5-month open competition | arXiv:2606.13591, June 11, 2026 | A — large-scale, open, reproducible | [...] | Capability | Ruflo v3.6.10 | SOTA 2026 | Gap |

| Knowledge retrieval | HNSW flat vector similarity (ReasoningBank) | KG graph traversal (Agents-K1, entity/claim/evidence edges) | Critical — architectural |
| Reasoning token interleaving | SONA adapts at trajectory end only | Interleaved think/act per tool boundary (arXiv:2505.19640) | High — implementation |
| Memory compression budget | None — all entries retained at full fidelity | Storage-budgeted LoRA compression (MemRefine) | Medium — implementation |
| Published eval harness | None | AgentBeats (298 judges, 467 subjects) | Visibility gap |
